source_license: Public — official government data. Verify terms of use with Loudoun County before commercial redistribution.
ordinance_version: See Municode for current version — a comprehensive zoning ordinance rewrite has been underway
pilot_notes: Loudoun County is the most important pilot jurisdiction. The Ashburn/Sterling area (Data Center Alley) is the largest data center market in the world by capacity. The county has a defined 'Data Center Facility' use and a Data Center Overlay District (DCOD). Dimensional standards and use classifications in this file are drawn from general public knowledge about Loudoun County zoning and require official verification against the current ordinance.
known_limitations: Zoning polygon geometry is demo only — run data/zoning/scripts/run_zoning_pipeline.py to download real geometry from Loudoun County ArcGIS.
Dimensional standards are approximate and require official verification against the current Loudoun County Zoning Ordinance.
Loudoun County has been revising its Zoning Ordinance — confirm which version is currently effective at https://www.loudoun.gov/zoning before relying on specific section numbers.

PD-IP (Planned Development - Industrial Park) development standards are largely governed by approved development plans and proffered conditions, which vary by parcel. Base ordinance standards are minimums only.
The Data Center Overlay District (DCOD) boundary requires official verification — contact the Loudoun County Department of Planning and Zoning.
Parcel-level data not yet integrated — precise district identification for a specific address requires parcel geometry.
Use table completeness is partial — only the most data-center-relevant districts are included in this pilot.
notes: Pilot jurisdiction — Phase 1 of zoning intelligence system. Demonstrates complete architecture: jurisdiction config, district records, dimensional standards, use classifications, overlay data, source tracking, and confidence levels.
